title: potentilla acaulis subsp. arenaria
intro: Potentilla acaulis subsp. arenaria, also known as the desert cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla acaulis subsp. arenaria include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
